claim_boundary: Ticket 26 closes small proof-kernel obligations and shortcut refutations. It does not prove or disprove RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

attempts.attempt: Close the logical micro-lemma behind Ticket 25: a finite prefix certificate cannot by itself prove an all-height RH-equivalent statement unless it is paired with a theorem covering the unchecked tail.
attempts.formal_micro_lemma_statement: If a proposed RH route verifies only a finite prefix P and supplies no theorem for n>P or heights outside the checked window, then the route is not a universal proof certificate.

micro_lemma_certificate.proof_reason: Universal quantification cannot be discharged by a finite prefix without a separate tail theorem.

attempts.remaining_obligation: A genuine RH proof still needs an all-height zeta-zero or equivalent positivity theorem.
attempts.claim_boundary: No RH proof and no certified RH counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Move the Ticket 25 Collatz affine-lift kernel from an empirical audit into an exact arithmetic micro-proof certificate.
attempts.formal_micro_lemma_statement: For an accelerated Collatz valuation word w of length k, total shift s, and affine constant c, any positive integer cycle realizing w must satisfy (2^s-3^k)n=c; hence 2^s>3^k, 2^s-3^k divides c, and the candidate n must replay the exact valuation word.

false_cycle_certificates.label: false_quotient_cycle_1
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [3, 1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1].
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

false_cycle_certificates.label: false_quotient_cycle_2
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2].
The composition is F_w(n)=(2187*n+3389)/2^9.
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

false_cycle_certificates.label: false_quotient_cycle_3
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1].
The composition is F_w(n)=(729*n+745)/2^7.
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Therefore (2^7-729)*n=745.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

positive_control_certificate.label: known_1_cycle
proof_steps: Compose the exact accelerated odd Collatz branches for word [2].
The composition is F_w(n)=(3*n+1)/2^2.
A cycle with this exact word must satisfy F_w(n)=n.
Therefore (2^2-3)*n=1.
Thus a positive integer cycle requires positive denominator, divisibility, and exact valuation replay.

attempts.closed_obligation: False quotient SCCs are not Collatz counterexamples unless they pass the affine fixed-point kernel.
attempts.remaining_obligation: The full Collatz conjecture still needs a global well-founded rank or cover for every non-cyclic exact branch and every larger quotient.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no Collatz counterexample found.

attempts.attempt: Close the finite-window shortcut: a verified Goldbach window is a certificate only on its stated domain and cannot be promoted to all even integers without an explicit large-even theorem.
attempts.formal_micro_lemma_statement: A Goldbach certificate over even integers 4<=n<=B does not decide any even n>B. A proof for all even n must combine the finite certificate with a separate theorem covering the tail.

micro_lemma_certificate.proof_reason: The universal claim ranges over infinitely many even integers outside the finite certificate.

attempts.remaining_obligation: A genuine Goldbach proof needs a positive two-prime representation lower bound beyond an explicit cutoff.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Goldbach proof and no Goldbach counterexample found in the certified finite window.

attempts.attempt: Close the bounded-gap shortcut: a statistic that remains positive after deleting all exact gap-2 pairs cannot by itself imply the twin prime conjecture.
attempts.formal_micro_lemma_statement: Any proof route whose decisive statistic is invariant under deleting exact gap-2 pairs while retaining bounded gaps proves at most a bounded-gap statement, not infinitely many exact twin pairs.
attempts.closed_obligation: Reject bounded-gap-only substitutions for twin-prime infinitude.
attempts.remaining_obligation: A genuine twin-prime proof needs an unconditional lower bound for exact gap 2 at arbitrarily large scale.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no Twin Prime counterexample.
